goal: Add lightweight Codex per-prompt Mainline context

summary.title: Codex per-prompt Mainline context
summary.what: Added Codex UserPromptSubmit hook output that refreshes lightweight Mainline state before each user prompt and injects it through hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext. The context includes a compact status summary and a capped proposals summary, while SessionStart keeps using the existing full session-start context path.
summary.why: Codex can inject model-visible context at user-prompt-submit time, unlike Cursor's current practical limitation around only relying on session-start context. Using that capability reduces the chance that the agent forgets active intent state or proposed-team-work awareness without moving semantic decisions into hook code.
summary.user_goal: Accept the proposed trade-offs: run lightweight status each prompt, include proposal ids/titles rather than full log, and implement/push the Codex hook enhancement on a new branch.

decisions.chose: Codex UserPromptSubmit runs only read-only Status and ListProposals and renders additionalContext.
decisions.rationale: This keeps prompt-time context fresh while preserving the rule that hooks do not decide whether to start, append, seal, or check intents.
rejected: Auto-start an intent from the prompt

decisions.chose: Render a compact status projection and at most five proposal id/title/thread/status entries.
decisions.rationale: This gives the agent enough signal to remember the workflow and spot proposed work without injecting full intent goals or the recent log every prompt.
rejected: Inject full mainline log on every prompt
Inject full proposal goals every prompt

decisions.chose: Extend the hook facade with read-only ListProposals.
decisions.rationale: Proposal ids/titles are deterministic state, not semantic output, and they let Codex render the accepted lightweight proposal summary.

rejected.alternative: Keep Codex identical to Cursor with session-start-only context
rejected.reason: Codex exposes UserPromptSubmit additionalContext, so ignoring it would leave useful fresh-state coverage unused.

rejected.alternative: Run mainline log/show automatically for every prompt
rejected.reason: That would increase latency and token usage and would duplicate the agent's explicit AGENTS.md-driven relevance judgment.

rejected.alternative: Let Stop hook infer whether a turn deserves append
rejected.reason: Meaningful logical change remains semantic and should stay with the agent.

risks: Codex hook output protocol may evolve; this relies on hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext for UserPromptSubmit remaining model-visible.
Every Codex prompt now performs two local read-only Mainline queries when hooks are enabled; status/list-proposals should stay cheap, but very slow storage could add prompt latency.
The per-prompt reminder still depends on the agent following the injected instructions; it improves recall but does not enforce semantic workflow completion.
followups: Consider a configurable cap for shown proposals if teams routinely have more than five active proposed intents.
If Codex adds reliable stop-continuation semantics, revisit a non-writing Stop reminder for dirty-worktree plus unappended-turn cases.

architectural_claims: Hook dispatcher remains limited to mechanical read-only state for prompt-time context; semantic workflow actions stay agent-driven.
Engine HookFacade now includes ListProposals as a read-only state query alongside Sync, Status, and BinaryStaleness.
Codex RenderHookOutput branches by hook name so SessionStart and UserPromptSubmit can produce different agent-protocol envelopes while other hooks stay silent.
behavioral_changes: Codex UserPromptSubmit hook now returns hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext containing compact Mainline per-prompt context when hooks are enabled.
Per-prompt context includes branch, actor, active intent, turn count, proposed count, sync stale flag, uncovered count, and up to five proposal ids/titles/threads/statuses.
Codex SessionStart continues to return the existing full Mainline session-start context.
Disabled hooks or missing engine still produce no Codex hook output.

api_changes.signature: Codex UserPromptSubmit hook stdout includes hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext when enabled

security_implications: Per-prompt context intentionally omits user prompt content and proposal goal text from the proposals summary to reduce repeated context exposure.
The new path reads local Mainline metadata only and does not send data to network endpoints beyond existing webhook behavior.
migration_notes: Existing Codex hook installations already include UserPromptSubmit; after rebuilding/reinstalling the mainline binary they will pick up the new output behavior.
